rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENA MAE RENFRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of progress, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative rulings for past hearings) since this petition was initially set for 12/06/2023: 1) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Esma Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 2) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of James Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 3) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Belinda Renfro (who survived the decedent and James Renfro and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. Note: Belinda Renfro's successors must sign two affidavits: one for the estate of James Renfro; and one for the estate of Belinda Renfro. See Probate Code sections 11801(a), 11802(c), and 13100 et seq; see also LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(2)(6). If a small estate affidavit cannot be obtained from all the successors (or cannot be used for any other reason), distribution must be made under section 11802(a). A certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative within 60 days before the hearing must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (CANNOT be e-filed), or a certification of authority for summary administration issued by a public administrator under section 7660(a)(2)(A) must be filed if distribution is made to the public administrator acting as personal representative to proceed with summary disposition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The status report filed on 03/18/2024 without a filing fee is not acceptable for the Court to take this status hearing off calendar. The status report must be re-filed with a hearing date on the caption page left blank for the clerk to assign a new hearing date upon payment of the required filing fee. See LRSF 14.35(J)(2). The administrator in pro per acting as his own counsel was so informed (via e-mail on 03/20/2024). It should also be noted that no partial or final inventory and appraisal has been filed. (In light of the many potential heirs, it would be helpful if courtesy copies of petitions and proofs of service were provided to the examiners.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND J. GIORDANI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/15/2024 (no earlier date available), as the supplement filed on 03/22/2024 did not address the following issues (notes e-mailed to counsel on 03/13/2024): 1) Please provide a proof that the estate cash on hand is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit under section 10533(a)(1) or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 10533(a)(2). Note: A review of an unfiled copy of the EL Dorado Savings Bank statement indicates that the estate cash is not fully insured. A portion of the estate cash in excess of the FDIC limit must be transferred to a different institution. 2) No timely election was filed by the surviving spouse under section 13502(a)(1) to probate 1/3 of the decedent's separate property otherwise passing to the spouse without administration under section 13500. No court approval has been sought for a late election under section 13502(b), nor has the petition requested relief under section 13502.5 to compel the administration of a fractional interest passing to the surviving spouse. Note: If the surviving spouse is not requeting court approval to make a late election, the petitioner must request an order under section 13502.5, with a showing of good cause required by that section, to administer a "fractional interest passing to the surviving spouse in any property in which the remaining fractional is subject to the administration".
